text: What is the author's main argument about the modern city?
zh: 作者关于现代城市的主要观点是什么？

text: In the organization of industrial life the influence of the factory upon the physiological and mental state of the workers has been completely neglected.
zh: 在工业生活的组织中，工厂对工人生理和心理状态的影响完全被忽视了。

text: Modern industry is based on the conception of the maximum production at lowest cost,
zh: 现代工业建立在以最低成本实现最大生产的理念之上，

text: in order that an individual or a group of individuals may earn as much money as possible.
zh: 为的是让个人或群体能够赚取尽可能多的钱。

text: It has expanded without any idea of the true nature of the human beings who run the machines,
zh: 工业的扩张从未考虑过操作机器的人类的真实本性，

text: and without giving any consideration to the effects produced on the individuals and on their descendants by the artificial mode of existence imposed by the factory.
zh: 也从未考虑过工厂所强加的这种人为生活方式对个人及其后代造成的影响。

text: The great cities have been built with no regard for us.
zh: 大城市的建造从未考虑过我们。

text: The shape and dimensions of the skyscrapers depend entirely on the necessity of obtaining the maximum income per square foot of ground,
zh: 摩天大楼的形状和尺寸完全取决于每平方英尺土地所能获得的最大收益，

text: and of offering to the tenants offices and apartments that please them.
zh: 以及提供能让租户满意的办公室和公寓。

text: This caused the construction of gigantic buildings where too large masses of human beings are crowded together.
zh: 这导致了巨型建筑的兴建，把过多的人群拥挤地聚集在一起。

text: Civilized men like such a way of living.
zh: 文明人却喜欢这种生活方式。

text: While they enjoy the comfort and banal luxury of their dwelling, they do not realize that they are deprived of the necessities of life.
zh: 他们享受着居所的舒适与平庸的奢华，却没有意识到自己被剥夺了生活的必需品。

text: The modern city consists of monstrous edifices and of dark, narrow streets full of petrol fumes and toxic gases,
zh: 现代城市由庞大怪异的建筑以及充满汽油味和有毒气体的黑暗狭窄街道组成，

text: torn by the noise of the taxicabs, lorries and buses, and thronged ceaselessly by great crowds.
zh: 被出租车、卡车和公共汽车的噪音撕裂，川流不息的人群不断涌动。

text: Obviously, it has not been planned for the good of its inhabitants.
zh: 显然，这座城市的规划从未考虑过居民的福祉。
